Records.description: One-loop three-point conformal integral in $D = 2$ with edge weights $\nu_i = 1/2$, obtained by reducing a 4-point conformal integral after sending $x_3$ to infinity. All internal lines are massless; external momenta taken Euclidean with $p_0^2 = -2, p_1^2 = -3, p_2^2 = -5$. The analytic result is expressible in terms of elliptic K-functions of single-valued complex cross ratios $z, \bar{z}$ (Corcoran et al. [2104.12776]). feyntrop reproduces the leading $\varepsilon^0$ value $9.97192$ at $\lambda=0, N=10^8$, illustrating fast Euclidean performance on an elliptic integrand. Called a '1-loop 4-point elliptic conformal integral' in the paper but reduced to 3-point form by conformal symmetry.
Records.familyName: One-loop three-point conformal triangle integral with half-integer edge weights (elliptic)
Records.location: Section 5.7 (An elliptic, conformal, 4-point integral), label sec:conformal, eq. (conformal_integral)

Records.familyName: massless 1-loop triangle with two off-shell legs

Records.description: Massless one-loop three-point auxiliary topology $A_1$ with three propagators, defined in Eq. (2.1) of Section 2. Used at the symmetric subtraction point $p_1^2=p_2^2=q^2=-1$. After IBP reduction with Reduze 2 it has 2 master integrals (Table 3, Table 4). Solved via differential equations in $z$ with $q^2=x p_1^2, x=-(z-1)^2/z$; DE brought to epsilon-form via package epsilon, integrated as GPLs on the six-root-of-unity alphabet. Results expanded through transcendental weight six and provided in the ancillary file anc/smom-f2u.
Records.familyName: massless 1-loop 3-point vertex at symmetric point (3 propagators)

Records.description: One-loop massless three-point vertex integrals at the SMOM symmetric point $p_1^2=p_2^2=q^2=-Q^2$, arising from the three-gluon, ghost-gluon, and quark-gluon vertex functions in QCD. Reduced to 2 master integrals via IBP.
Records.familyName: one-loop three-point SMOM symmetric-point massless vertex
Records.location: Sec. II (Details of the calculation), p.2
